title: sidalcea calycosa subsp. rhizomata
intro: Sidalcea calycosa subsp. rhizomata, also known as the Western marsh marigold, is a perennial plant that belongs to the family Malvaceae. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The plant's health benefits include soothing irritated skin, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing. Sidalcea calycosa subsp. rhizomata can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
